title: Mayor Walsh Announces Repaving West Genesee Street Near Downtown
summary: Mayor Ben Walsh announced a West Genesee Street repaving project near downtown Syracuse beginning September 11, 2023, involving milling and paving from North Clinton Street to West Street. The project is part of the "Dig Once" initiative coordinating with utility providers to replace underground infrastructure and minimize future excavations, with work expected to complete in one week during daytime hours while keeping roads open.

political_signal.description: Mayor publicly championing coordinated infrastructure investment that reduces future disruption and demonstrates efficient use of public resources through Dig Once coordination model.

processing_notes: Author listed as 'Unknown' in source metadata; actual author is City Communications Office
Date discrepancy: Title header shows 2024 breadcrumb but article date is 2023-09-08; content is internally consistent with September 2023
Sharon F. Owens appears in page header navigation but is not mentioned in article body; Ben Walsh is the acting Mayor in this announcement
No budget figures provided; no dollar amounts extracted
Utility coordination aspect suggests involvement of multiple infrastructure systems (water, sewer, telecom, gas) but specific departments not named
